talk_metadata.session_title: El Camino de la Pediatria: Finding Our Way Through the Chasm of Pediatric Oncology Drug Development
talk_metadata.talk_title: Small molecules and ADCs in pediatric oncology
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the trial was activated in December of 2024 and closed eight months later"
Speaker said: "we need to get trials open because the most important thing I think we have as academic pediatric oncologists is the ability to generate the data faster"
Speaker said: "there were nine trials of folate receptor alpha in ADCs that were open, and eight of them were in adults, one of them was in children"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of patient selection based on quantitative folate receptor alpha expression levels
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Three of these 10 children in the monotherapy only arm had complete responses

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Only a few pediatric-specific arms; most ADC trials enroll adults and add children later, diluting pediatric signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative CD33 expression threshold for response
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small sample size (10 patients) limits statistical power

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: GLOBE BNHL platform trial (CD19‑directed ADC in NHL)
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: All‑comer age criteria without separate pediatric cohort
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Mixed adult/child enrollment may mask pediatric efficacy

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the only pediatric trial relied on an expanded access experience, suggesting the core ADC scaffold is not uniquely protected for pediatrics
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Company ran out of money before completing pediatric trial, indicating manufacturing/financing bottleneck
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on adult‑first development and late pediatric entry delays signal generation

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Development timeline shows long gap between adult pivotal trial and pediatric phase‑1, highlighting dependence on adult data
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Sequential adult‑then‑pediatric approach lengthens time to pediatric approval

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high pre‑clinical HER2 mRNA/IHC expression in osteosarcoma but concluded that the HER2‑ADC trial failed to meet the bar for expansion, despite a patient with low HER2 having prolonged stable disease

key_data_gaps: No overall survival data for most ADC single‑arm trials
Absence of pediatric‑only randomized comparator arms

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a quantitative folate receptor alpha expression assay to select pediatric patients most likely to benefit from FRα‑ADC therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we could generate data faster... the trial closed early... three of ten children had complete responses"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate RNA‑seq and proteomics to define expression thresholds and predict responders

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify and target downstream resistance pathways (e.g., drug efflux, payload detoxification) in FRα‑ADC non‑responders
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted limited durability of responses and early trial termination
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model tumor‑cell signaling networks to pinpoint compensatory pathways for combination therapy

crispro_opportunity.description: Propose pairing FRα‑ADC with immune checkpoint blockade to enhance efficacy in pediatric AML
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed using ADC as a bridge to transplant and the need for combination strategies
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate synergistic effects of ADC + anti‑PD‑1 based on tumor microenvironment profiles

cited_competitors.name: Seattle Genetics
cited_competitors.context: Developed brentuximab vedotin, an ADC cited as a successful adult‑first example

cited_competitors.name: AstraZeneca
cited_competitors.context: Developing a CD2‑directed ADC with a topoisomerase payload to reduce hepatotoxicity for children

cited_competitors.name: SUTRO Biopharma
cited_competitors.context: Company behind the folate receptor alpha ADC that ran out of money and closed early

unresolved_questions: How can pediatric ADC trials be funded sustainably without relying on adult commercial success?
What quantitative biomarker thresholds should define eligibility for FRα‑ADC therapy?
Can multi‑institutional pediatric ADC trials be harmonized to reduce variability?

talk_metadata.session_title: El Camino de la Pediatria: Finding Our Way Through the Chasm of Pediatric Oncology Drug Development
talk_metadata.talk_title: Precision immunotherapy: CAR T cells and bispecific antibodies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "there have been no additional approvals of CAR T cells for pediatric cancer since tisagenlecleucel in 2017"
Speaker said: "the results are exciting but not currently being explored or FDA‑approved for pediatric patients"
Speaker said: "individual academic centers each manufacturing their own product raises consistency concerns"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Antigen loss (CD19 negative relapse) not addressed in single‑target CAR designs
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker noted that some patients lose CD19 expression as a mechanism of resistance
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: High response rates in CD19‑naïve patients but lower durability in relapsed cases

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Various single‑institution CAR T trials (e.g., GD2 CAR in neuroblastoma)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: GD2 expression in neuroblastoma and diffuse midline glioma
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Trials often limited to single centers with heterogeneous manufacturing, making cross‑patient comparisons difficult
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Standardized GD2 density cutoff for eligibility
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial biopsies to track antigen modulation over time
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small cohort sizes (e.g., 4‑8 patients) limit statistical power

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Lack of unified eligibility criteria across sites, leading to variable disease burden at enrollment
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Heterogeneous endpoints and lack of control arms

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that each institution uses different vectors, cytokine cocktails, and growth conditions, creating variability
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Manufacturing cost ~ $50,000 per patient and limited to centers with GMP facilities
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: No centralized regulatory pathway; reliance on institutional trials hampers broad access

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Although approved, the therapy lacks a formal companion diagnostic for CD19 density, limiting precise patient selection
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Continued off‑label use in younger ages without age‑specific formulation

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed 81% remission rate in the single‑arm tisagenlecleucel trial but concluded that lack of FDA approval for most pediatric CAR T products limits broader patient access

key_data_gaps: No randomized pediatric CAR T trials for solid tumors
Absence of long‑term overall survival data beyond 3 years for many constructs
Lack of standardized manufacturing quality metrics across sites

crispro_opportunity.description: Design dual‑target CARs (CD19 + CD22) guided by CrisPRO modeling of antigen co‑expression to prevent escape
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "dual targeting CAR showed 86% to 99% response rates"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict optimal antigen pairings and simulate escape dynamics

crispro_opportunity.description: Create a pan‑cancer expression atlas for GD2, CD22, and other CAR targets to define quantitative thresholds for enrollment
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted lack of standardized GD2 density cutoff in neuroblastoma trials
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO integrates bulk and single‑cell RNA‑seq to generate actionable expression cutoffs

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO’s computational pipeline to standardize CAR construct design and predict manufacturing robustness across centers
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted variability in constructs and manufacturing processes across institutions
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model vector design, cytokine milieu, and predict functional potency before GMP production

cited_competitors.name: Novartis
cited_competitors.context: Developer of tisagenlecleucel, the only FDA‑approved pediatric CAR T therapy

cited_competitors.name: Kite Pharma
cited_competitors.context: Developed CD19 CARs referenced in discussion of antigen loss resistance

cited_competitors.name: Juno Therapeutics
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned in context of CD22‑CAR trials with 70‑75% response rates

unresolved_questions: What regulatory framework can enable multi‑institutional academic CAR T products to achieve consistent FDA approval?
How can long‑term safety and durability be monitored across disparate manufacturing sites?
What is the optimal timing for introducing CAR T therapy in the treatment algorithm to maximize efficacy and minimize toxicity?
